text: Setting clear goals is essential for any project to succeed. Using the SMART framework helps you create goals that are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. Once your goals are defined, break the project down into smaller milestones—these are key deadlines that help you track progress. Each milestone should lead to a concrete deliverable, such as a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by sharing these goals and milestones with everyone involved, including clients and team members. When all stakeholders agree on the objectives, the project runs more smoothly and avoids misunderstandings later on.
